commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=0c00a78623fa6c2aec7b5d48e3a67dd033cb2377)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded-agent model-call diagnostics to compute `responseStreamBytes` from stream chunks after stripping `partial` snapshots and adds focused regression tests for delta-only counting and uninspectable chunks.

PR surface: Source +19, Tests +104. Total +123 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main stringifies every streamed chunk, and standard stream events carry cumulative `partial` snapshots on deltas and end events. I did not rerun the Windows local-provider starvation scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a maintainer compatibility/API decision for the published diagnostics metric, not a safe automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): count stream deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- fixes the diagnostics stream-byte counter introduced by `c6e98493511b3a18e6f2f402693ef7dc832a071f` so plain stream deltas are counted from the new `delta` only
- avoids serializing full `partial` assistant snapshots on every streamed chunk, which removes the repeated answer-so-far work called out in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599#issuecomment-4555471713
- keeps diagnostic byte inspection best-effort so opaque or hostile chunk shapes cannot break the model stream

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: model-call diagnostics no longer repeatedly stringify full accumulated assistant partial snapshots for ordinary `text_delta`, `thinking_delta`, and `toolcall_delta` stream chunks.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate on the refreshed branch, focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree after final rebase, formatter/lint/diff checks, and branch autoreview.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts --reporter=dot
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main...HEAD
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main
node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 45m --ttl 90m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"
```

Evidence after fix: focused local wrapper passed `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.model-diagnostic-events.test.ts` with 13/13 tests after the final rebase to `642f85dc5b9`; oxfmt, oxlint, and diff checks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings; AWS Crabbox `check:changed` passed on the refreshed branch with provider `aws`, lease `cbx_7e1be9a155f0`, slug `pearl-shrimp`, run `run_addc72e8ce2b`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit 0, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `core` and `coreTests`.

Observed result after fix: regression coverage proves diagnostic byte accounting does not call `toJSON` on full partial snapshots for text deltas, counts only incremental delta bytes, and keeps streaming alive when byte inspection cannot inspect a chunk.

What was not tested: Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This removes the diagnostics amplification from the starvation cluster but does not claim to close every related local-provider responsiveness path alone.
target.author: vincentkoc

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
